info.title: Video Script Schema
info.description: Schema for generating faceless technical video scripts. The output JSON drives the full rendering pipeline: voice synthesis, stock footage download, data chart rendering, and FFmpeg compositing. Use this schema to produce valid payloads that the engine can render into 1080p/30fps videos.

VideoScript.description: Top-level video script. Contains a title and an ordered list of scenes that get rendered and stitched into the final video.

title.description: Video title. Used for file naming and displayed in the UI. Keep concise and descriptive. Example: 'NVIDIA vs AMD: The GPU Revenue War (2024)'.

scenes.description: Ordered list of scenes. Each scene becomes one video clip. Aim for 5-15 scenes per video. Scenes are stitched in scene_number order with transitions between them.

Scene.description: A single scene in the video. Combines narration audio with a visual element. The narration_text drives scene duration (~150 words/minute).

scene_number.description: 1-based sequential scene order. Must start at 1 and increment by 1. Controls the order scenes appear in the final video.

narration_text.description: Voiceover text spoken during this scene. This text is synthesized to audio and drives scene duration. Keep between 20-60 words for good pacing (roughly 8-24 seconds). Write in a natural, conversational tone — not robotic or overly formal.

emotion.description: Emotional tone of the narration. Affects voice synthesis parameters (speed, pitch, emphasis). Match to content: 'analytical' for data breakdowns, 'excited' for surprising numbers, 'curious' for questions, 'serious' for warnings.

VisualInstruction.description: Defines what the viewer sees during a scene. The 'type' field determines which rendering pipeline is used and which fields are valid inside 'data'.

type.description: The visual scene type. This is the most important field — it controls whether the scene shows stock footage, a data chart, a code snippet, etc. Choose based on what best communicates the scene's content.

title.description: Short label for this scene. Used as heading in text overlays, chart titles, and for internal identification. Keep under 60 characters.

transition.description: Transition effect when cutting FROM the previous scene INTO this one. Vary between scenes for visual interest. Use 'smooth*' between related data scenes, 'circle*' for reveals, 'fade*' for topic changes. Don't use the same transition for every scene.

data.description: Type-specific configuration. The valid fields depend entirely on the 'type' value. See the discriminated union under DataByVisualType for exact field specs per type.

VisualType.description: Determines the rendering pipeline for this scene. Choose the type that best matches the scene content.

x-enum-descriptions.stock_video: B-roll footage with just a title overlay. Use when the footage itself tells the story. Minimal text. Rendering: downloads stock footage from Pexels based on keywords.
x-enum-descriptions.stock_with_text: B-roll footage with heading + body text overlay. Default choice for intro, outro, and narrative scenes. Rendering: downloads stock footage, overlays styled text.
x-enum-descriptions.stock_with_stat: B-roll footage with a single large stat value + label + subtitle. Use for highlighting one key number (e.g. '$3.1T Market Cap'). Rendering: downloads stock footage, overlays large stat.
x-enum-descriptions.stock_quote: B-roll footage with a quote + attribution overlay. Use for expert quotes, user comments, or notable statements. Rendering: downloads stock footage, overlays styled quote.
x-enum-descriptions.social_card: Styled social media post mockup (Reddit/Twitter/HN). Use when referencing a specific social media post or comment. Rendering: renders UI mockup over dark background.
x-enum-descriptions.data_chart: Animated data visualization (bar, line, donut, timeseries, etc). Use for ANY numerical data, comparisons, trends, or financial metrics. Supports live Yahoo Finance auto-fetch for stock data. Rendering: modern infographic style with 3-phase animation (axis construction → data reveal → labels/badges).
x-enum-descriptions.code_snippet: Syntax-highlighted code block on dark background. Use when showing actual code, terminal commands, or configuration. Rendering: dark theme code block with syntax colors.
x-enum-descriptions.text_overlay: Simple text on dark background. No stock footage. Use for simple title cards or when no visual is needed. Rendering: white text on dark background.
x-enum-descriptions.section_title: Large section divider title. Use between major sections of the video to signal a topic change. Rendering: large animated title text.
x-enum-descriptions.bullet_reveal: Animated bullet point list. Use for listing features, steps, pros/cons, or key points. Rendering: bullets appear one by one with animation.
x-enum-descriptions.stat_callout: Large animated stat number on dark background. No stock footage. Use for dramatic single-number reveals. Rendering: large number with label, animated entrance.
x-enum-descriptions.quote_block: Styled quote on dark background. No stock footage. Use for quotes when you don't want B-roll behind it. Rendering: styled quote text with attribution.
x-enum-descriptions.comparison: Side-by-side comparison layout. Use for A vs B comparisons. Rendering: split-screen layout with items on each side.
x-enum-descriptions.fullscreen_statement: Single bold statement filling the screen. Use for dramatic emphasis or key takeaways. Rendering: large bold text, centered.
x-enum-descriptions.reddit_post: Reddit post mockup. Mapped to social_card internally with Reddit styling. Use specifically for Reddit references.

Emotion.description: Emotional tone of the narration. Affects voice synthesis speed, pitch, and emphasis. Match to the scene content for natural delivery.

x-enum-descriptions.neutral: Default, matter-of-fact delivery. Use when no specific emotion is needed.
x-enum-descriptions.curious: Asking questions, exploring ideas. Use for 'have you ever wondered...' moments.
x-enum-descriptions.confident: Stating facts, presenting data with authority. Use for strong claims backed by data.
x-enum-descriptions.excited: Revealing surprising data, big numbers, breakthroughs. Use for 'wow' moments.
x-enum-descriptions.surprised: Unexpected findings, plot twists in the data. Use for genuinely surprising reveals.
x-enum-descriptions.analytical: Breaking down numbers, explaining methodology. Use for deep dives and data analysis.
x-enum-descriptions.impressed: Reacting to impressive stats or achievements. Use for acknowledging remarkable results.
x-enum-descriptions.thoughtful: Reflecting on implications, drawing conclusions. Use for wrap-up and takeaway scenes.
x-enum-descriptions.serious: Warnings, risks, important caveats. Use for cautionary or high-stakes content.
x-enum-descriptions.humorous: Light moments, funny observations. Use sparingly for comic relief.
x-enum-descriptions.skeptical: Questioning claims, debunking. Use for 'but is it really...' moments.
x-enum-descriptions.dramatic: Building tension, cliffhangers. Use for dramatic reveals and suspense.

Transition.description: FFmpeg xfade transition effect when cutting from the previous scene into this one. Vary these between scenes for visual interest.

clean.description: Safe defaults. 'fade' works for any scene. 'fadeblack' for dramatic pauses between sections.

smooth.description: Flowing between related scenes. Best for sequences of data charts or related content.

directional.description: Scene changes with a sense of direction/progression. Good for moving through a timeline.

geometric.description: Eye-catching transitions. 'circleopen' is great for reveals. Use for emphasis.

reveal.description: Unveiling new information. Good for chart reveals and data presentations.

cover.description: One scene sliding over another. Good for topic changes.

stylized.description: Creative/tech feel. 'pixelize' is great for tech content. Use sparingly.

glitch.description: Edgy/modern feel. Use very sparingly for emphasis only.

ChartType.description: Determines which chart visualization to render inside a data_chart scene.

x-enum-descriptions.bar: Vertical bar chart with colored bars and value labels on top. Best for comparing discrete categories (e.g. company revenues, product sales).
x-enum-descriptions.line: Line chart with smooth thick lines. Best for trends over labeled periods (e.g. yearly revenue, quarterly growth). Use 'labels' for x-axis.
x-enum-descriptions.area: Same as line but with filled area beneath. Best when you want to emphasize volume/magnitude.
x-enum-descriptions.horizontal_bar: Horizontal bars sorted top to bottom. Best for rankings and leaderboards (e.g. market cap ranking, top 10 lists).
x-enum-descriptions.grouped_bar: Multiple colored bars side by side per category. Best for comparing multiple series across categories (e.g. Q1-Q4 revenue for 3 products). Requires 'series' field.
x-enum-descriptions.pie: Pie chart with percentage labels. Best for showing parts of a whole. Use when ≤6 segments. Prefer 'donut' when you have a total to show.
x-enum-descriptions.donut: Donut chart with center value/label and legend. Best for parts-of-whole with a summary stat in the center (e.g. '$60.9B Total Revenue'). Always include center_value and center_label.
x-enum-descriptions.timeseries: Date-based line chart with formatted date axis and optional event markers. Best for stock prices, monthly metrics, historical trends with actual dates. Supports Yahoo Finance auto-fetch via ticker/tickers fields. Use when x-axis represents real dates.

TextPosition.description: Controls where the text overlay appears on the video frame.

x-enum-descriptions.center: Centered horizontally and vertically. Default and most common choice.
x-enum-descriptions.top: Centered horizontally, near the top of the frame.
x-enum-descriptions.bottom: Centered horizontally, near the bottom. Good when footage has action in the upper area.
x-enum-descriptions.top_left: Top-left corner. Good for secondary info.

BRollDensity.description: Controls how many stock footage clips are downloaded and jump-cut together for a scene.

x-enum-descriptions.low: Single clip, minimal cuts. Calm, focused feel. Default for most scenes.
x-enum-descriptions.medium: 2 clips with a cut. Moderate pacing.
x-enum-descriptions.high: 3 clips with jump cuts. Fast-paced, energetic feel. Best for hooks and intros.

SocialPlatform.description: Determines the UI mockup style for social_card scenes.

x-enum-descriptions.reddit: Reddit post/comment UI mockup with upvotes, subreddit, username.
x-enum-descriptions.twitter: Twitter/X post UI mockup with likes and retweets.
x-enum-descriptions.hackernews: Hacker News post UI mockup with points and comments.

StockVideoData.description: Data for stock_video scenes. Use when the footage itself tells the story with minimal overlay. Generate this when type='stock_video'.

keywords.description: 2-3 descriptive search phrases for Pexels stock footage. Be specific and visual — 'server room blue lights' is better than 'technology'. NEVER use generic terms like 'man in office' or 'business meeting'.

title.description: Optional title text overlaid on the video. If omitted, uses the scene's visual_instruction.title.

StockWithTextData.description: Data for stock_with_text scenes. B-roll footage with heading + body text overlay. Default choice for intro/outro/narrative scenes. Generate this when type='stock_with_text'.

heading.description: Large heading text overlaid on the video. Keep under 40 characters for readability on screen.

body.description: Smaller subtitle text below the heading. Keep under 80 characters. Can be empty string.

keywords.description: 2-3 descriptive Pexels search phrases. Be specific and visual. NEVER use generic terms.

position.description: Where the text appears on screen. Default 'center'.

broll_density.description: How many stock clips to use. Use 'high' for hooks/intros, 'low' for calm scenes.

StockWithStatData.description: Data for stock_with_stat scenes. B-roll footage with a single large stat value + label. Use for highlighting one key number. Generate this when type='stock_with_stat'.

value.description: The stat value displayed large on screen. Pre-formatted — displayed as-is. Include formatting like commas, %, +, $. Examples: '22,000+', '$3.1T', '460%', '14 cores', '220,000°C'.

label.description: Description of what the value represents. Displayed below the value in smaller text. Example: 'Multi-Core Geekbench Score'.

subtitle.description: Additional context line below the label. Good for comparisons like '25% faster than M3 Pro'.

keywords.description: Pexels search keywords for background footage. Be specific and visual.

StockQuoteData.description: Data for stock_quote scenes. B-roll footage with a quote + attribution overlay. Use for expert quotes or notable statements. Generate this when type='stock_quote'.

quote.description: The quote text. Displayed in large styled text with quotation marks. Keep under 150 characters for readability.

attribution.description: Who said it and where. Format: '— Name, Title/Source'. Example: '— Jensen Huang, NVIDIA CEO'.

keywords.description: Pexels search keywords for background footage.

SocialCardData.description: Data for social_card scenes. Renders a styled social media post mockup. Use when referencing a specific social media post or comment. Generate this when type='social_card' or type='reddit_post'.

platform.description: Social platform. Determines the UI mockup style (Reddit upvotes, Twitter likes, etc).

username.description: Display username. Include platform prefix: 'u/' for Reddit, '@' for Twitter. Example: 'u/chipfanatic'.

post_title.description: Post title (mainly for Reddit). Can be empty for Twitter-style posts.

body.description: The post/comment body text. Keep under 200 characters for readability on screen.

upvotes.description: Upvote/like count displayed on the card. Use realistic numbers.

comments.description: Comment count displayed on the card.

subreddit.description: Subreddit name (Reddit only). Include 'r/' prefix. Example: 'r/technology'.

DataChartData.description: Data for data_chart scenes. Renders animated data visualizations with modern infographic style. Supports TWO MODES: (1) Static data — provide labels + values/series directly. Use for custom data like revenue breakdowns. (2) Yahoo Finance auto-fetch — provide ticker/tickers + period, leave values/series empty. Renderer fetches live price data automatically. Use for stock prices and market comparisons. PREFER auto-fetch for any publicly traded data. Generate this when type='data_chart'.

chart_type.description: Which chart to render. Choose based on what story the data tells. Use 'timeseries' for date-based data (stock prices), 'bar' for category comparisons, 'donut' for parts-of-whole, 'line' for trends with label-based x-axis.

labels.description: X-axis labels or category names. Required for static data mode (bar, line, horizontal_bar, grouped_bar, pie, donut). Not needed when using Yahoo Finance auto-fetch. Keep labels short (under 15 chars each).

values.description: Single series numeric values. Must be same length as labels. Use raw numbers for unit='$' — formatting is automatic (e.g. 60900000000 renders as '$60.9B'). Use 'series' instead for multi-series charts. Leave empty/omit when using Yahoo Finance auto-fetch.

series.description: Multiple named data series. Use instead of 'values' for multi-series line, grouped_bar, or timeseries charts. Each series gets a different color automatically. Leave empty/omit when using Yahoo Finance auto-fetch.

unit.description: Value unit for axis formatting. Set to '$' for automatic currency formatting ($1.5K, $2.3M, $60.9B). Set to '%' when using value_type='pct_change'. Leave empty for plain numbers.

y_label.description: Y-axis label text. Examples: 'Revenue (USD)', 'Price per Share', 'Units Sold', 'YoY Revenue Growth %'.

subtitle.description: Explanatory text shown at the bottom of the chart. Use for context like 'Fiscal Year 2024', '+460% growth in 5 years', or 'Percentage change over 2 years'. Always include for credibility.

source.description: Data source attribution shown bottom-left in small text. Always include for credibility. Auto-set to 'Yahoo Finance' when using ticker mode. Examples: 'SEC Filings', 'Yahoo Finance', 'Company 10-K', 'ASML Annual Reports'.

duration.description: Override scene duration in seconds. If omitted, duration is calculated from narration_text word count (~150 words/minute). Only set this if you need a specific duration.

center_value.description: DONUT ONLY. Large text displayed in the center of the donut hole. Pre-formatted — displayed as-is. Examples: '$60.9B', '78%', '€38.8B'. Always include for donut charts.

center_label.description: DONUT ONLY. Small text below center_value. Describes what the center value represents. Examples: 'Total Revenue', 'Market Share', 'Order Backlog'. Always include for donut charts.

dates.description: TIMESERIES ONLY (static mode). Date strings for the x-axis. Supported formats: 'YYYY-MM-DD', 'YYYY-MM', 'YYYY'. Must be in chronological order. The renderer auto-formats axis labels. Not needed when using Yahoo Finance auto-fetch.

series_name.description: TIMESERIES ONLY (single series static mode). Name for the single series shown in legend. Example: 'NVIDIA Revenue'.

events.description: TIMESERIES ONLY. Key events to mark on the chart as dashed vertical lines with label badges. Use for product launches, earnings dates, market events. Keep to 2-4 events max with labels under 20 characters.

ticker.description: YAHOO FINANCE AUTO-FETCH. Single stock ticker symbol. The renderer fetches live closing price history from Yahoo Finance at render time. No dates/values needed. Example: 'NVDA', 'ASML', 'AAPL'. Use for single-line stock price charts.

tickers.description: YAHOO FINANCE AUTO-FETCH. Multiple ticker symbols for comparison charts. Each becomes a separate colored line/series. Max 5 tickers. Example: ['NVDA', 'AMD', 'INTC']. Best with value_type='pct_change' when comparing stocks at different price levels.

period.description: YAHOO FINANCE AUTO-FETCH. Lookback period for price history.

x-enum-descriptions.max: All available history. Best with interval='1mo'.

interval.description: YAHOO FINANCE AUTO-FETCH. Data point interval. Choose based on period to avoid noisy charts: '1d' for 1mo-3mo, '1wk' for 6mo-2y, '1mo' for 5y+.

x-enum-descriptions.1d: Daily data points. Use for short periods (1mo-3mo). Too noisy for longer periods.
x-enum-descriptions.1wk: Weekly data points. Default. Good balance for 6mo-2y periods.
x-enum-descriptions.1mo: Monthly data points. Use for long periods (5y+). Smoothest lines.

value_type.description: YAHOO FINANCE AUTO-FETCH. How to display fetched values.

x-enum-descriptions.close: Raw closing price in USD. Default. Use for single-ticker price charts. Set unit='$'.
x-enum-descriptions.pct_change: Percentage change indexed from the start date (0%). Use when comparing tickers at different price levels (e.g. NVDA at $800 vs INTC at $30). Each line starts at 0% and shows relative performance. End-of-line badges show total % change.

ChartSeries.description: A named data series for multi-series charts (line, grouped_bar, timeseries).

name.description: Series name shown in the chart legend. Examples: 'NVIDIA', 'Company A', 'Product X'.

values.description: Numeric data points for this series. Must be same length as labels/dates array.

ChartEvent.description: A key event marker on a timeseries chart. Rendered as a dashed vertical line with a label badge.

date.description: Date of the event. Same format as the dates array (YYYY-MM-DD or YYYY-MM). Example: '2024-03'.

label.description: Short text shown next to the vertical marker line. Keep under 20 characters. Example: 'Blackwell announced'.

CodeSnippetData.description: Data for code_snippet scenes. Renders syntax-highlighted code on dark background. Use for showing actual code, terminal commands, or configuration. Generate this when type='code_snippet'.

code.description: The actual code to display. Use \n for newlines. Keep under 15 lines for readability on screen.

title.description: Optional title shown above the code block.

TextOverlayData.description: Data for text_overlay scenes. Simple text on dark background with no stock footage. Use for title cards or when no visual is needed. Generate this when type='text_overlay'.

text.description: The text to display on screen.

BulletRevealData.description: Data for bullet_reveal scenes. Animated bullet points that appear one by one. Use for listing features, steps, key points, pros/cons, or timelines. Generate this when type='bullet_reveal'.

bullets.description: The bullet points to reveal one by one. Keep each bullet under 50 characters. 3-6 bullets is ideal.

title.description: Heading shown above the bullet list.

ComparisonData.description: Data for comparison scenes. Side-by-side A vs B layout. Use for comparing two options, technologies, or approaches. Generate this when type='comparison'.

left_title.description: Title for the left column. Example: 'DUV (Nikon/Canon)'.

right_title.description: Title for the right column. Example: 'EUV (ASML)'.

left_items.description: Items/features for the left side. Keep each item concise.

right_items.description: Items/features for the right side. Keep each item concise.

FullscreenStatementData.description: Data for fullscreen_statement scenes. Single bold statement filling the screen. Use for dramatic emphasis, key takeaways, or closing lines. Generate this when type='fullscreen_statement'.

statement.description: The text displayed large and centered on screen. Keep under 60 characters for maximum impact. Example: 'ASML: The starting line, the track, and the finish line.'

StatCalloutData.description: Data for stat_callout scenes. Large animated stat number on dark background (no stock footage). Use for dramatic single-number reveals when you don't want B-roll behind it. Generate this when type='stat_callout'.

value.description: The stat displayed very large on screen. Pre-formatted. Examples: '$3.1T', '460%', '50,000'.

label.description: What the stat represents. Displayed below the value. Example: 'NVIDIA Market Cap'.

subtitle.description: Additional context. Example: 'Briefly surpassed Apple in 2024'.

SectionTitleData.description: Data for section_title scenes. Large section divider. Use between major sections of the video to signal a topic change. Generate this when type='section_title'.

heading.description: Large section title text. Example: 'Part 2: The Data'.

subtitle.description: Smaller text below the heading. Example: 'Let\'s look at the numbers'.

QuoteBlockData.description: Data for quote_block scenes. Styled quote on dark background (no stock footage). Use for quotes when you don't want B-roll behind it. Generate this when type='quote_block'.

attribution.description: Who said it. Format: '— Author Name'. Example: '— Jensen Huang'.

x-generation-rules.description: Rules the LLM MUST follow when generating a VideoScript JSON payload.
rules: Every scene MUST have scene_number (sequential from 1), narration_text, emotion, and visual_instruction.
visual_instruction MUST have type, title, transition, and data.
Use data_chart whenever presenting numerical data, comparisons, or trends. Do NOT describe numbers in text overlays when a chart would be clearer.
Use timeseries chart_type when x-axis represents actual dates. Use line when x-axis is just labels (years, quarters without specific dates).
PREFER Yahoo Finance auto-fetch (ticker/tickers) over hardcoded values for any publicly traded stock, ETF, or index data. This keeps charts current.
When comparing stocks at different price levels, use value_type='pct_change' so the chart shows relative performance.
For Yahoo Finance charts, choose interval based on period: '1d' for 1mo-3mo, '1wk' for 6mo-2y, '1mo' for 5y+.
For unit='$', values MUST be raw numbers (e.g. 60900000000 not '60.9B') — the renderer formats automatically.
For data_chart, always include source attribution and subtitle for context.
For donut charts, always include center_value and center_label.
Keep narration_text between 20-60 words per scene (~8-24 seconds).
Vary transitions between scenes. Don't use fade for every scene.
Match emotion to narration tone: analytical for data, excited for surprises, curious for questions.
Keywords for stock footage NEVER generic like 'man in office'. Use specific visual terms.
Output ONLY the JSON object. No markdown, no explanation, no code fences.
